name: Corn & Potato Chowder
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large heavy saucepan, heat the oil and sherry until bubbling.
2. Add onion and sauté 5 minutes. If the mixture appears dry, add 1-2 teaspoons of water.
3. Stir in carrot, celery, bay leaf, potatoes, and stock. Cover and bring to boil.
4. Reduce heat to medium and cook for 10-15 minutes, or until potatoes are tender.
5. Add milk and corn; simmer for 3 minutes, or until corn is tender. Discard the bay leaf.
6. Puree 1 cup of soup in a blender, then return to the pot.
7. Season with cayenne. If desired, garnish with yogurt.
Note: Use caution when adding cayenne. The first time I made this, I added too much and it was quite spicy. I omitted the yogurt, and the dish still tasted fine.
